title: Rewriting Bun in Rust
friendlyTitle: Bun used AI to help rewrite itself in Rust
summary: Bun creator Jarred Sumner wrote that Bun is being rewritten from Zig to Rust to improve stability and systematically prevent memory-safety bugs. He disclosed using a pre-release Claude Fable 5 heavily during the rewrite, reaching a passing test suite across platforms in 11 days.
friendlySummary: The Bun JavaScript tool is moving from Zig to Rust to improve reliability. Its creator says Claude helped complete much of the rewrite very quickly.
whyItMatters: This is a concrete example of AI changing software maintenance, not just greenfield coding. If one experienced engineer plus coding agents can safely accelerate large rewrites, teams may revisit migrations and reliability work that previously looked too expensive.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows how AI can help with big software upgrades that used to take much longer.
